mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures contemporaneous consumer spending strength, which directly flows into aggregate corporate revenue and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between monthly RSAFS changes and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is not positive and statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1600 thousand units signal a contraction in residential investment and construction employment, reducing aggregate demand and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1600, or the mean return in those months exceeds the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal a labor market deterioration that compresses household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month that are larger than either signal in isolation.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases fails to produce lower average S&P 500 20-day returns than either condition alone (t-test p > 0.05 for difference in means).

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary policy regimes, month-over-month increases in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) force the central bank to maintain or raise the federal funds rate, elevating equity discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Within FEDFUNDS > 4% regimes, month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes show no negative correlation with S&P 500 5-day forward returns (Rank IC > -0.02 over a 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects manufacturing and utilities output expansion, signaling higher near-term corporate earnings and capacity utilization that drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Monthly INDPRO changes fail to exhibit a positive Spearman correlation with S&P 500 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.5 percent indicates slack labor markets that reduce wage growth and consumer spending confidence, triggering risk-averse equity positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of triggered instances (UNRATE > 5.5%), or the mean return in those instances is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) revises aggregate income and corporate profit expectations upward; higher-than-trend GDP prints produce positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as analysts update earnings models.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth surprises show no statistically significant positive correlation with subsequent S&P 500 20-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.5 over any 3-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Restrictive federal funds rates combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) tighten mortgage credit and construction finance simultaneously, transmitting a broad credit crunch that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition of month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases and HOUST declines fails to produce lower average S&P 500 60-day returns than independent additive effects (interaction term t-stat > -1.5 in pooled regression).
